revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:第六天榊神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakaki Shrine |image = File:DairokutenSakakiJinja.JPG |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}{{ill|Kuramae|ja|蔵前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramae|1=Kuramae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621884}} 1-chōme 4-3 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|00.83|N|139|47|21.38|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title|qq=}} |Enshrined Deity = Sakaki Imperial Great Deity (also known as the Sixth Heavenly Sakaki Deity) |Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Alternate Names = Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine, Dairokuten Shrine, Dairokuten Shrine, Dairokuten Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361236}} |Annual Festival = First {{ill|Saturday|en|Saturday|qid=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|Sunday|en|Sunday|qid=Q132|lt=Sunday|1=Sunday}} of June |deity = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|阿夜訶志古泥神|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}}/{{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|ja|天魔|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=Sixth Heavenly Demon King|lt_ja=第六天魔王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taitō-ku|qid=Q232641|1=Taitō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.700231|N|139.789272|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine''' (第六天榊神社) == Overview == It is said that during the time of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} conducted his campaign against the eastern barbarians, the shrine was established. Although it had long been called '''Dairokuten Jingū''', in the 6th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}) the shrine’s name was changed to '''Sakaki Shrine''' based on its former title.<ref>[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/syoukai/04_taitou/4015.html Sakaki Shrine] (Tokyo {{ill|Shinto Shrines Administration|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrines Administration|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590703}}: {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}})</ref> In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} era ({{ill|1928|en|1928|qid=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}}), it relocated to the site of the former {{ill|Tokyo Higher Technical School|ja|東京高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Higher Technical School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京工業大学|1=Tokyo Institute of Technology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q587326}} (previously {{ill|Tokyo Vocational School|ja|東京職工学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Vocational School|1=Tokyo Vocational School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17229281}}, now {{ill|Tokyo Institute of Technology|en|Tokyo Institute of Technology|qid=Q587326|lt=Tokyo Institute of Technology|1=Tokyo Institute of Technology}}) and remains there (see [[#Location]] for details). The shrine is one of the stops on the {{ill|Shitamachi Hachifukujin|ja|下町八福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitamachi Hachifukujin|1=Shitamachi hachifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361236}} pilgrimage course. == Deities == * Main Deity: Sakaki Imperial Great Deity (also known as the Sixth Heavenly Sakaki Deity) ** {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt_ja=面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qq=}} ** Hōkon Son (惶根尊) == Shrine Grounds == On the southern side (in front of the worship hall) and on the western side of the grounds stand two {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}, with a shrine signboard installed beside the southern torii. Entering through the southern torii, the area immediately to the right in front of the worship hall is the {{ill|Chozuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chozuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=Chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1069667}}. Furthermore, within the shrine grounds there are the following sub-shrines, as well as a {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|qid=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}}, a treasure house, and a well (currently not for drinking), among other structures. === Sub-shrines in the Grounds === * Shichifuku {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|Kotohiraga Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|lt=Jihira Shrine|lt_ja=事比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohiraga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrines|qid=Q11646488}} ・ {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}} {{Disambiguation needed|date=January 2016|qq=}} * Flourishing Inari Shrine === Gallery === ; Torii, Shrine Signboard, and Chozuya <gallery widths="210px" heights="160px"> File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja02.JPG|Front (Southern) Torii File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja01.JPG|Western Torii File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja03.JPG|Shrine Signboard (beside the front torii) File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja04.JPG|Chozuya </gallery> ; Sub-shrines in the Grounds <gallery widths="210px" heights="160px"> File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja06.JPG|Shichifuku Inari Shrine File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja07.JPG|Jihira Shrine and Toyouke Shrine File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja08.JPG|Flourishing Inari Shrine </gallery> ; Others <gallery widths="210px" heights="160px"> File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja09.JPG|Treasure House File:Dairokuten-Sakaki-Jinja10.JPG|Well </gallery> == Festivals == * The annual festival is held on the first {{ill|Saturday|en|Saturday|qid=Q131|lt=Sat|1=Saturday}} and {{ill|Sunday|en|Sunday|qid=Q132|lt=Sunday|1=Sunday}} of June. The grand festival takes place every other year<ref>[http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/06%20dairokutensakaijinjareitaisai.html Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine Grand Festival] (Tokyo Festivals)</ref>. ** The shrine's mikoshi is a {{ill|Senkan Mikoshi|ja|千貫神輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senkan Mikoshi|1=sengan mikoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406811}} with a platform wheel width of 4 shaku 2 sun, second only in size to the main mikoshi of the nearby {{ill|Torikoshi Shrine|ja|鳥越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torikoshi Shrine|1=Torikoe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675149}}. == Parish Area == : Entire 1-chōme of {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}} {{ill|Kuramae|ja|蔵前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramae|1=Kuramae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621884}}, 2-chōme numbers 1–13, 3-chōme numbers 1–5, 7, 9, 12, 13, and 4-chōme numbers 1–17, 19–22 : In {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Asakusabashi|ja|浅草橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusabashi|1=Asakusa Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18611483}} 1-chōme: 9, 18, 30; 3-chōme: 18–28 : In {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Yanagibashi|ja|柳橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagibashi|1=Yanagibashi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11534347}} 1-chōme: 1, 5, 9–16, 20, 22, 25–31; 2-chōme: 16–22 : In {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Shou (character)|en|Shou (character)|qid=Q1181540|lt=Kotobuki|1=Shou (character)}} 3-chōme: 1–16 == Relocation History == * During the Edo period, the shrine moved to Kuramae when the mountain in {{ill|Torikoshi|ja|鳥越|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torikoshi|1=Torikoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675130}} was excavated to obtain soil for reclamation. * In the 4th year of the {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} era ({{ill|1719|en|1719|qid=Q6902|lt=1719|1=1719}}), it relocated to the present site in 1-chōme of Yanagibashi. * In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} era ({{ill|1928|en|1928|qid=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}}), it moved to its current location (see [[#Location]] for details). == Location == * Current: {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}{{ill|Kuramae|ja|蔵前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramae|1=Kuramae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621884}} 1-chōme 4-3 * The current site was once home to a government-run {{ill|Library|en|Library|qid=Q7075|lt=library|1=Library}} known as {{ill|Asakusa Bunko|ja|浅草文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Bunko|1=Asakusa Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557225}}. Asakusa Bunko closed in May of the 14th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}}), and the site subsequently became the campus of {{ill|Tokyo Vocational School|ja|東京職工学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Vocational School|1=Tokyo Vocational School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17229281}} (later {{ill|Tokyo Higher Technical School|ja|東京高等工業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Higher Technical School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京工業大学|1=Tokyo Institute of Technology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q587326}}, now {{ill|Tokyo Institute of Technology|en|Tokyo Institute of Technology|qid=Q587326|lt=Tokyo Institute of Technology|1=Tokyo Institute of Technology}}). Following the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3742341}}, when Tokyo Higher Technical School relocated to {{ill|Meguro|en|Meguro|qid=Q233903|lt=Meguro|1=Meguro}} {{ill|Ōokayama|ja|大岡山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōokayama|1=Ōokayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434728}}, a portion of the former campus (near the school's main gate) became the new site of the shrine in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} era (1928), where it remains today. === Access === * Approximately a 6-minute walk from {{ill|Toei Asakusa Line|en|Toei Asakusa Line|qid=Q720653|lt=Toei Asakusa Line|1=Toei Asakusa Line}} and {{ill|Toei Ōedo Line|en|Toei Ōedo Line|qid=Q384862|lt=Toei Ōedo Line|1=Toei Ōedo Line}} station {{ill|Kuramae Station|en|Kuramae Station|qid=Q112348|lt=Kuramae Station|1=Kuramae Station}}. * Approximately a 6-minute walk from {{ill|Toei Asakusa Line|en|Toei Asakusa Line|qid=Q720653|lt=Toei Asakusa Line|1=Toei Asakusa Line}} and {{ill|Chūō-Sōbu Line|en|Chūō-Sōbu Line|qid=Q1091295|lt=JR Chūō-Sōbu Local Line|1=Chūō-Sōbu Line}} station {{ill|Asakusabashi Station|en|Asakusabashi Station|qid=Q800404|lt=Asakusabashi Station|1=Asakusabashi Station}}. == References == {{Reference Help|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} =
== See Also === {{Commonscat|Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Torikoshi Shrine|ja|鳥越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torikoshi Shrine|1=Torikoe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675149}} * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} – Present in various locations mainly in {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655841}}. == External Links == * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/taito/3223 Sakaki Shrine] (Tokyo {{ill|Shinto Shrines Administration|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrines Administration|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590703}}: {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}) * {{Twitter|dai6tensakaki|Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine|qq=}} Official Account * [http://jinjajin.jp/modules/newdb/detail.php?id=110 Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine] ({{ill|Shrine Person|ja|神社人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Person|1=Shrine Person|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590698}}) * [http://goshuin.ko-kon.net/touto_jinja/06_dairokuten_sakaki.html Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine] (Kokon Religious Research Institute) * [http://www.tesshow.jp/taito/shrine_kuramae_sakaki.html Dairokuten Sakaki Shrine] (Neko no Ashiato) {{Shinto stub|qq=}}
{{Dairokuten Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|第六天榊神社|version=93738145|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:第六天榊神社]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Dairokuten Sakaki jinjya}} {{wikidata link|Q11602398}} [[Category:Dairokuten Shrines]] [[Category:Kuramae]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]
